title: Chunked Transfer Coding, Extensions, Trailers, and Decoding · Go Build Room
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first incremental chunked decoder and trailer policy for chunked transfer coding, extensions, trailers, and decoding. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of incremental chunked decoder and trailer policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse bounded hexadecimal chunk size with extensions, overflow checks, exact CRLF, and cumulative decoded limits.
Read exactly each chunk-data length plus its terminating CRLF across arbitrary fragmentation.
Treat the last zero-sized chunk as the transition to trailer parsing rather than immediate message completion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Chunk Size Hex Overflow
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 1864 in a municipal gateway receiving one-byte TCP fragments. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound chunk accumulation and require trailer validation after the zero chunk; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Chunk Size Hex Overflow.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for parse bounded hexadecimal chunk size with extensions, overflow checks, exact crlf, and cumulative decoded limits. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Chunk Size Hex Overflow, keep Chunk Size Hex Overflow connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

hints: For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Chunked Changed Corpus, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Chunked Changed Corpus precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Chunked Changed Corpus, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A workshop protocol team handles case 1872 in a archival client parsing incomplete close-delimited responses. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound chunk accumulation and require trailer validation after the zero chunk; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test extensions, leading zeros, split size lines, missing CRLF, overflow, excess trailer bytes, forbidden fields, and a pipelined next message.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Chunked Changed Corpus

Build deterministic Go evidence to bound chunk accumulation and require trailer validation after the zero chunk; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only.
